title: 💰 Mega Backdoor Roth: $46K Tax-Free Strategy for 2026
description:
How to stuff up to 46,000 dollars extra per year into Roth accounts using the mega backdoor Roth strategy. After tax 401k contributions, in service rollovers, plan eligibility check, and step by step execution.

Sources: IRS Notice 2025-83 contribution limits, IRS Publication 525, Plan Sponsor Council of America 2024 survey.

segments.voice: In 8 minutes you will know exactly how to legally contribute up to 46,000 dollars per year extra into Roth accounts using the mega backdoor Roth strategy. This is not a tax loophole. This is the way Congress and the IRS designed the rules. Only about 22 percent of 401k plans currently allow the mega backdoor. If yours does, ignoring it leaves hundreds of thousands of tax free dollars on the table over a career. Today we cover the eligibility check, execution steps, and traps to avoid.
segments.visual: Mega backdoor Roth strategy 46000 dollars after tax contribution diagram

segments.voice: The 2026 contribution math that makes this work. The 401k employee deferral limit is 24,500 dollars under age 50. The total annual addition limit per plan is 70,000 dollars in 2026 per IRS Notice 2025-83. That includes employer match. If your employer matches 4 percent of a 200,000 dollar salary, that is 8,000 dollars. Subtract your 24,500 employee deferral and 8,000 employer match from the 70,000 plan limit. The remaining 37,500 dollars is the mega backdoor space available to you.
segments.visual: 70000 plan limit 24500 employee 8000 match 37500 mega backdoor space

segments.voice: Three plan features must align for the mega backdoor to work. First, your 401k plan must allow after tax contributions. This is different from Roth 401k contributions. After tax goes into a separate after tax bucket inside the plan. Second, your plan must allow in service distributions or in plan Roth conversions while you are still employed. Without this, the after tax money grows tax deferred but you cannot move it to a Roth structure. Third, the plan administrator must process the rollovers efficiently.
segments.visual: Three requirements after tax contributions in service distribution plan check

segments.voice: How to check if your plan offers the mega backdoor. Step one, download your summary plan description from your benefits portal. Step two, search for the phrase after tax contributions. Many plans list this as voluntary contributions or non Roth after tax. Step three, search for in service distribution or in service withdrawal. Step four, if both terms appear and the document confirms the features are available, call your plan administrator to verify. The 401k call center reps are often confused, so ask them to confirm in writing.
segments.visual: Summary plan description benefits portal search after tax in service feature

segments.voice: Companies known to offer the mega backdoor include Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, Netflix, Salesforce, and most large tech and finance firms. Federal employees in the Thrift Savings Plan do not currently have access. Most small business 401k plans do not offer it because of administrative complexity. The Plan Sponsor Council of America 2024 survey found that 51 percent of large plans with over 5000 participants offer mega backdoor versus only 18 percent of plans with under 100 participants.
segments.visual: Microsoft Google Amazon Meta Netflix logos mega backdoor employer support

segments.voice: The mechanics of after tax contributions. After tax money is contributed from your paycheck using post tax dollars. There is no tax deduction. The money sits in the after tax bucket inside the 401k and grows tax deferred. The growth on after tax contributions is taxed as ordinary income when withdrawn, which is why you want to convert to Roth as fast as possible to lock in the tax free growth treatment going forward.
segments.visual: After tax 401k bucket post tax dollars no deduction tax deferred growth

segments.voice: The in service rollover step. Most plans that allow after tax contributions also allow you to either roll the after tax money to a Roth IRA outside the plan or convert it to a Roth 401k inside the plan. The in plan Roth conversion is the cleanest option because it stays inside your 401k and avoids paperwork. Some plans automate this with a feature called automatic Roth in plan conversion that happens daily or weekly on every after tax contribution.
segments.visual: In service rollover Roth 401k in plan conversion automatic daily weekly

segments.voice: Why automatic conversion is critical. If after tax money sits for months before conversion, it accumulates investment growth. That growth is treated as pre tax money. When you eventually convert, the pre tax growth portion is taxable as ordinary income. The taxable amount can be hundreds or thousands of dollars per year depending on market returns. Automatic same day or same week conversion eliminates this problem because there is no time for growth to accumulate.
segments.visual: Automatic conversion daily after tax pre tax growth elimination tax problem

segments.voice: Step by step execution at Microsoft as an example. Microsoft has used Fidelity as the 401k record keeper for years. The Microsoft 401k allows after tax contributions and offers automatic daily in plan Roth conversion. An eligible employee logs into NetBenefits, navigates to contribution rates, sets after tax percentage to capture the full mega backdoor space, and enables the automatic conversion option. Once set, every paycheck deduction flows from paycheck to after tax bucket to Roth 401k automatically.
segments.visual: Microsoft NetBenefits Fidelity 401k after tax contribution rate automatic Roth

segments.voice: Step by step execution at Google. Google calls this feature the Google after tax 401k with Roth conversion. The plan is administered by Vanguard. Employees can contribute up to the full plan limit after employee deferral and employer match are accounted for. The conversion happens automatically. Google publishes internal documentation explaining the mega backdoor as part of standard benefits onboarding. Many other tech companies have adopted similar approaches.
segments.visual: Google Vanguard 401k after tax Roth conversion automated benefits onboarding

segments.voice: The rollover to external Roth IRA alternative. If your plan allows in service distributions instead of in plan conversion, you roll the after tax money out to a Roth IRA at Vanguard, Fidelity, or Schwab. This requires manual quarterly or annual paperwork. The advantage is your money goes into a Roth IRA where you control the investment options and avoid future 401k plan changes. The disadvantage is the administrative friction and the risk of forgetting to do the rollover.
segments.visual: In service distribution to external Roth IRA Vanguard Fidelity quarterly paperwork

segments.voice: The IRS Notice 2014-54 ruling that made the mega backdoor crystal clear. The IRS published guidance in 2014 confirming that when a 401k distribution includes both pre tax and after tax portions, the participant can direct each portion to different destinations. After tax to Roth IRA, pre tax to traditional IRA. This eliminated ambiguity that previously caused plan administrators to refuse split rollovers. Without Notice 2014-54, the mega backdoor would still be theoretical.
segments.visual: IRS Notice 2014-54 ruling split rollover after tax pre tax destinations

segments.voice: How much extra Roth space the mega backdoor creates. Take a Microsoft engineer earning 350,000 dollars per year. Employee deferral 24,500. Employer match approximately 9,500. After tax contribution to fill 70,000 limit, 36,000 dollars converted to Roth 401k. Over a 25 year career, that 36,000 per year times 25 years equals 900,000 dollars of additional Roth principal contributions. With 8 percent compounding, the total Roth balance from this strategy alone exceeds 2.5 million dollars tax free.
segments.visual: Microsoft engineer 25 year career 36000 yearly Roth 2.5 million dollars

segments.voice: The mega backdoor versus the regular backdoor Roth. The regular backdoor Roth IRA gives you 7,500 dollars per year of after tax Roth space. The mega backdoor 401k gives you up to 46,500 dollars per year depending on your salary, match, and employee deferral level. That is 6 times more Roth space. Both strategies stack. A high earner couple can use the regular backdoor for 15,000 dollars and the mega backdoor for another 70,000 dollars across both spouses 401ks if both have eligible plans.
segments.visual: Mega backdoor versus regular backdoor 46500 vs 7500 dollars comparison

segments.voice: Common mistake number one. Confusing after tax contributions with Roth 401k contributions. They are different buckets with different rules. Roth 401k contributions are limited to the 24,500 employee deferral limit. After tax contributions are limited only by the 70,000 plan limit minus other contributions. You can do both in the same year. Roth 401k for the first 24,500 of contributions and after tax for everything above that up to the plan limit.
segments.visual: After tax vs Roth 401k bucket confusion separate contribution types limits

segments.voice: Common mistake number two. Not enabling the automatic conversion option when available. If your plan supports automatic Roth conversion of after tax contributions but the default is off, you must turn it on. Many people contribute to after tax for a year before realizing they never enabled the conversion. The growth accumulated during that year becomes permanently pre tax money that triggers taxes at conversion. Check and enable automatic conversion immediately.
segments.visual: Plan settings automatic conversion enable default off mistake configuration

segments.voice: Common mistake number three. Missing the contribution timing. The 70,000 plan limit applies on a calendar year basis. If you front load your employee deferrals early in the year, you may hit the 24,500 limit by July and stop receiving any per pay period match the rest of the year. Coordinate with payroll to spread contributions across the full year to maximize match while also funding the after tax bucket.
segments.visual: Calendar year contribution pacing front load match timing payroll coordination

segments.voice: Common mistake number four. Ignoring the highly compensated employee testing rules. The IRS requires 401k plans to pass non discrimination tests showing that benefits do not favor highly compensated employees over rank and file workers. After tax contributions are subject to actual contribution percentage testing. If too few lower paid employees contribute after tax, the plan may force refunds of excess contributions to highly compensated employees in March of the following year.
segments.visual: HCE testing non discrimination ACP refund highly compensated employee rules

segments.voice: Common mistake number five. Cashing out the mega backdoor money when changing jobs. When you leave the employer, you can roll the entire 401k including the Roth converted mega backdoor portion to a Roth IRA at any brokerage. Some people accidentally take a cash distribution which triggers ordinary income tax on the pre tax portion and 10 percent early withdrawal penalty if under 59 and a half. Always direct rollover to an IRA, never take a check made out to you.
segments.visual: Job change 401k rollover direct vs cash distribution penalty tax mistake

segments.voice: The self employed mega backdoor solo 401k variant. Self employed individuals can set up a solo 401k that allows after tax contributions and in plan Roth conversions. The administrator must offer this feature, which not all do. Mysolo401k.net, Discount Solo 401k, and a few specialized providers offer plans that support the strategy. The 70,000 dollar plan limit still applies, but solo 401ks have only your contributions and the employer profit sharing which gives more space for after tax.
segments.visual: Self employed solo 401k mega backdoor mysolo401k.net discount providers

segments.voice: Coordination with the regular backdoor Roth IRA. The mega backdoor 401k does not interfere with the regular backdoor Roth IRA. They use completely different rules and limits. A high earner couple can execute both strategies in the same year. Wife does regular backdoor 7,500 plus mega backdoor through her 401k. Husband does regular backdoor 7,500 plus mega backdoor through his 401k. Combined Roth contributions can exceed 100,000 dollars in a single year.
segments.visual: Combined backdoor Roth IRA mega backdoor 401k coordination couple high earner

segments.voice: The investment selection inside the after tax bucket. Most plans give you the same investment menu for after tax contributions as for pre tax contributions. Choose the lowest cost broad market index fund available. Vanguard Total Stock Market Index, Fidelity 500 Index Fund FXAIX, and Schwab Total Stock Market Index are common choices with expense ratios under 0.05 percent. Keep the strategy simple and let the tax advantage do the heavy lifting.
segments.visual: Investment menu index funds Vanguard Fidelity Schwab low cost selection

segments.voice: Why the mega backdoor matters more than ever. The federal debt has crossed 38 trillion dollars in 2026. The Congressional Budget Office projects that absent significant policy changes, tax rates will need to rise substantially over the coming decades. Locking in tax free Roth space at today known rates is one of the most valuable hedges against future tax increases. Each dollar of mega backdoor Roth converted today is permanently shielded from future rate hikes.
segments.visual: Federal debt 38 trillion dollars 2026 CBO tax rate projections Roth hedge

segments.voice: But here is what nobody talks about. The mega backdoor Roth is on the political target list. Multiple congressional proposals over the last 5 years have attempted to eliminate or limit the strategy. So far, all have failed. But this could change. The window to maximize this strategy may not be permanent. Use it aggressively while it remains available. Tax law uncertainty argues for converting more, not less, while the rules are clear.
segments.visual: Congressional proposals limit mega backdoor political risk window uncertainty

segments.voice: Your action plan. Step one, download your 401k summary plan description and verify the mega backdoor is available. Step two, calculate your maximum after tax contribution space based on salary, deferral, and match. Step three, enable after tax contributions and automatic Roth conversion through your benefits portal. Step four, choose a low cost index fund for the after tax investments. Step five, verify the conversions happen automatically each pay period. Next video, the HSA triple tax advantage that makes it the most powerful retirement account nobody talks about.
segments.visual: Action plan checklist mega backdoor Roth execution steps verification 2026
